title: How Far Can Wealth Effects Go?
text:
How Far Can Wealth Effects Go?

Tan Kai Xian

Gavekal Research | 10 Jun 2026

The state of the US job market and household wealth effects are the main drivers of US consumption. Since January 2024, the latter has done the heavy lifting. US disposable personal income growth slowed from 6.8% year-on-year in January 2024 to 2.6% in April 2026. Over the same period, consumption growth rose modestly from 5.0% to 5.8% as the personal savings rate fell from 6.4% to 2.6%. In other words, US consumers continue to expand their outlays on goods and services by spending a larger share of their income (see chart below).
